task.title: CadQuery hex_nut generator + ISO 4032 catalog data
task.why: Only hex_bolt, washer, retaining_ring exist. hex_nut is mecsu.vn's second most common product. Any request for hex-nut-* raises KeyError. Must add CadQuery generator, mock template, registry entry, and catalog data.

step.code:
""" Hex Nut mock template — ISO 4032. CadQuery actual generation is in backends.py CadQueryBackend._hex_nut(). """ import json from app.cad.template_base import CadTemplate, GeneratedModel
class HexNutTemplate(CadTemplate): family = "hex_nut" supported_standards = ["ISO4032", "DIN934", "GB6170"] required_params = ["d", "s", "m"]
def generate(self, params: dict, fmt: str, quality: str) -> GeneratedModel: self.validate_params(params) payload = { "generator": "mock", "template": "hex_nut", "format": fmt, "quality": quality, "params": params, "geometry_plan": { "hex_body": f"polygon(6, s={params['s']}) × h={params['m']}mm", "bore": f"circle(d/2={float(params['d'])/2})", "chamfer": "both faces", }, } return GeneratedModel( content=json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, indent=2).encode("utf-8"), format=fmt, metadata=payload, )

implementation.step:
Add _hex_nut() to CadQueryBackend and register in generate() dispatch. Also FIX existing _hex_bolt() — current code puts head UP and shank DOWN creating an X-shape. Correct: head at Z=0..head_height, shank at Z=head_height..total_length.
# ── In CadQueryBackend.generate() — add hex_nut to dispatch ───────── elif family == "hex_nut": shape = self._hex_nut(params)
# ── Fix _hex_bolt() — correct geometry orientation ────────────────── def _hex_bolt(self, params: dict[str, Any]): for key in ["d", "L", "P", "k", "s", "b"]: if key not in params: raise ValueError(f"Missing required params for hex_bolt: {key!r}")
d = float(params["d"]) total_length = float(params["L"]) head_height = float(params["k"]) across_flats = float(params["s"]) thread_length = float(params["b"]) pitch = float(params["P"])
# CadQuery polygon() takes circumradius (across_corners / 2) # across_corners = across_flats / cos(30°) = across_flats / 0.866025 circumradius = across_flats / 0.866025 # = across_corners shank_radius = d / 2 grip_length = total_length - thread_length minor_radius = shank_radius - 0.6134 * pitch # ISO 68-1 minor diameter
# 1. Hex head: Z = 0 → head_height head = ( self.cq.Workplane("XY") .polygon(6, circumradius) .extrude(head_height) ) try: head = head.faces(">Z").chamfer(min(1.0, head_height * 0.12)) head = head.faces("<Z").chamfer(min(0.5, head_height * 0.08)) except Exception: pass
# 2. Grip (smooth shank): Z = head_height → head_height + grip_length grip = ( self.cq.Workplane("XY") .workplane(offset=head_height) .circle(shank_radius) .extrude(grip_length) )
# 3. Thread section: Z = head_height+grip_length → head_height+total_length thread_start = head_height + grip_length thread_core = ( self.cq.Workplane("XY") .workplane(offset=thread_start) .circle(minor_radius) .extrude(thread_length) )
bolt = head.union(grip).union(thread_core)
# 4. Thread helix (medium/high LOD only) lod = str(params.get("lod", "medium")) if lod in ("medium", "high"): try: segments = 32 if lod == "high" else 16 helix_solid = self._thread_helix( major_radius=shank_radius, minor_radius=minor_radius, pitch=pitch, length=thread_length, z_start=thread_start, segments=segments, ) bolt = bolt.union(helix_solid) except Exception: pass # helix can fail on edge-case sizes; thread_core remains
return bolt
def _hex_nut(self, params: dict[str, Any]): """ISO 4032 hex nut. params: d (nominal), s (across flats), m (height).""" for key in ["d", "s", "m"]: if key not in params: raise ValueError(f"Missing required params for hex_nut: {key!r}")
d = float(params["d"]) across_flats = float(params["s"]) nut_height = float(params["m"])
circumradius = across_flats / 0.866025 bore_radius = d / 2
# 1. Hex body nut = ( self.cq.Workplane("XY") .polygon(6, circumradius) .extrude(nut_height) )
# 2. Chamfer both faces try: chamfer_size = min(0.8, nut_height * 0.1) nut = nut.faces(">Z").chamfer(chamfer_size) nut = nut.faces("<Z").chamfer(chamfer_size) except Exception: pass
# 3. Bore: cut through hole nut = ( nut.faces(">Z") .workplane() .circle(bore_radius) .cutThruAll() )
return nut
def _thread_helix( self, major_radius: float, minor_radius: float, pitch: float, length: float, z_start: float, segments: int = 16, ) -> "cq.Workplane": """ ISO 68-1 simplified thread helix for visual fidelity. 60° thread angle, swept in XZ plane. Falls back gracefully — caller wraps in try/except. """ # Thread profile in XZ plane (will be swept along helix) profile = ( self.cq.Workplane("XZ") .moveTo(minor_radius, 0) .lineTo(major_radius, pitch * 0.25) .lineTo(major_radius, pitch * 0.75) .lineTo(minor_radius, pitch) .close() ) mid_radius = (major_radius + minor_radius) / 2 helix = self.cq.Wire.makeHelix(pitch, length, mid_radius) thread = ( self.cq.Workplane("XY") .workplane(offset=z_start) .add(self.cq.Solid.sweep(profile.val(), [helix])) ) return thread

Add hex-nut-iso4032 to CATALOG and VARIANTS dicts. ISO 4032 data — all dimensions in mm, source: ISO 4032:2012 Table 1.
# ── ADD to CATALOG dict ───────────────────────────────────────────── "hex-nut-iso4032": Product( product_id="hex-nut-iso4032", standard="ISO4032", family="hex_nut", name="Đai ốc lục giác ISO 4032", parameters=[ ParameterSpec(name="d", label="Đường kính danh nghĩa (mm)", values=[6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 20, 24]), ParameterSpec(name="s", label="Rộng 2 cạnh song song (mm)"), ParameterSpec(name="m", label="Chiều cao đai ốc (mm)"), ], ),
# ── ADD to VARIANTS dict ──────────────────────────────────────────── # ISO 4032 Table 1: d, s (across flats), m (nut height), all in mm "hex-nut-iso4032": [ { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m6", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M6", "label": "M6", "diameter_label": "M6", "params": {"d": 6, "s": 10.0, "m": 5.2}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m8", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M8", "label": "M8", "diameter_label": "M8", "params": {"d": 8, "s": 13.0, "m": 6.8}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m10", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M10", "label": "M10", "diameter_label": "M10", "params": {"d": 10, "s": 17.0, "m": 8.4}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m12", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M12", "label": "M12", "diameter_label": "M12", "params": {"d": 12, "s": 19.0, "m": 10.8}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m14", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M14", "label": "M14", "diameter_label": "M14", "params": {"d": 14, "s": 22.0, "m": 12.8}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m16", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M16", "label": "M16", "diameter_label": "M16", "params": {"d": 16, "s": 24.0, "m": 14.8}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m20", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M20", "label": "M20", "diameter_label": "M20", "params": {"d": 20, "s": 30.0, "m": 18.0}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, { "variant_id": "hex-nut-iso4032-m24", "sku": "HEX-NUT-ISO4032-M24", "label": "M24", "diameter_label": "M24", "params": {"d": 24, "s": 36.0, "m": 21.5}, "material": "carbon_steel", }, ],

tests.code:
import pytest from app.cad.registry import template_registry from app.cad.backends import get_cad_backend from fastapi.testclient import TestClient from app.main import app
client = TestClient(app)
cadquery_unavailable = False try: import cadquery # noqa: F401 except ImportError: cadquery_unavailable = True
def test_hex_nut_template_registered(): t = template_registry.get_by_family("hex_nut") assert t.family == "hex_nut" assert "ISO4032" in t.supported_standards
def test_hex_nut_registry_by_product_id(): t = template_registry.get_by_product("hex-nut-iso4032") assert t.family == "hex_nut"
def test_hex_nut_lock_nut_maps_to_hex_nut(): t = template_registry.get_by_product("lock-nut-din985") assert t.family == "hex_nut"
def test_hex_nut_variants_endpoint(): r = client.get("/api/v1/products/hex-nut-iso4032/variants") assert r.status_code == 200 data = r.json() assert data["total"] >= 6 assert "M8" in data["grouped_by_diameter"] m8 = data["grouped_by_diameter"]["M8"][0] assert m8["params"]["d"] == 8 assert m8["params"]["s"] == 13.0 assert m8["params"]["m"] == 6.8
def test_hex_nut_mock_generate(): backend = get_cad_backend("mock") result = backend.generate("hex-nut-iso4032", {"d": 8, "s": 13.0, "m": 6.8}, "glb", "preview") assert b"hex_nut" in result.content
@pytest.mark.skipif(cadquery_unavailable, reason="cadquery not installed") def test_cadquery_hex_nut_returns_valid_glb(): backend = get_cad_backend("cadquery") result = backend.generate("hex-nut-iso4032", {"d": 8, "s": 13.0, "m": 6.8}, "glb", "preview") assert result.content[:4] == b"glTF", "Not a valid GLB file" assert len(result.content) > 2000
@pytest.mark.skipif(cadquery_unavailable, reason="cadquery not installed") def test_cadquery_hex_bolt_geometry_is_upright(): """Bolt head and shank must not overlap (X-shape bug).""" backend = get_cad_backend("cadquery") result = backend.generate("hex-bolt-iso4014", {"d": 8, "L": 30, "P": 1.25, "k": 5.3, "s": 13, "b": 22, "lod": "medium"}, "glb", "preview") assert result.content[:4] == b"glTF" assert len(result.content) > 5000

task.commit_message: feat(T07): hex_nut CadQuery generator + ISO 4032 catalog + fix hex_bolt orientation
